description: Integrate private S3-hosted video samples and annotation metadata into the Claru portal catalog. Presigned URLs for secure video access, MetaTable for structured metadata rendering, tag-based filtering, CSV ingestion pipeline, annotation caching, extensible data panel system, and admin CSV import UI.

userStories.title: S3 Presigner Utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a utility to generate presigned URLs for S3 objects so the portal can serve private video/JSON files.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/s3/presigner.ts with getS3SignedUrl(objectKey: string, expiresIn?: number): Promise<string | null>
Uses @aws-sdk/client-s3 and @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner (already installed)
Reads S3_BUCKET_NAME and AWS_REGION from process.env
Default expiry: 3600 seconds
Returns null and logs error on failure
Handles keys with spaces and special characters (no manual encoding needed — SDK handles it)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: AWS SDK packages already installed. Env vars AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_REGION, S3_BUCKET_NAME already in .env.local.

userStories.title: S3 Presigned URL API Route
userStories.description: As a portal user, I need an authenticated API endpoint that returns presigned URLs so the browser can stream S3 videos.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/api/portal/s3-signed-url/route.ts with POST handler
Request body validated with Zod: { objectKey: string }
Response: { signedUrl: string } on success, { error: string } on failure
Validates Supabase session via createSupabaseServerClient() — returns 401 if unauthenticated
Rejects objectKey containing '..' (path traversal) — returns 400
Logs request: objectKey + user ID + timestamp via console.log
Returns 500 with generic message on AWS SDK failure
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Import getS3SignedUrl from src/lib/s3/presigner.ts (US-001). Follow pattern from existing admin API routes for auth checking.

userStories.title: S3 Annotation Fetch Utility and API Route
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a utility to fetch annotation JSON from S3 and an API route to serve it to the portal.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/s3/annotation.ts with fetchAnnotationJson(objectKey: string): Promise<Record<string, unknown> | null>
Generates presigned URL via getS3SignedUrl, fetches via global fetch(), parses JSON
10-second timeout using AbortController
Returns null and logs warning on failure (network error, invalid JSON, timeout)
Create src/app/api/portal/s3-annotation/route.ts with POST handler
Request body validated with Zod: { objectKey: string }
Validates Supabase session — returns 401 if unauthenticated
Rejects objectKey containing '..' — returns 400
Returns parsed JSON on success, { error: string } on failure
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reuse getS3SignedUrl from US-001. Pattern matches US-002 API route.

userStories.title: Database Migration: Add tags and S3 columns
userStories.description: As a developer, I need new database columns to support S3-referenced samples and tag-based filtering.
acceptanceCriteria: Create supabase/migrations/004_s3_integration.sql that adds: tags text[] DEFAULT '{}' to datasets table
Add s3_object_key text (nullable) to dataset_samples table
Add s3_annotation_key text (nullable) to dataset_samples table
Add s3_specs_key text (nullable) to dataset_samples table
Existing RLS policies on dataset_samples automatically cover new columns (SELECT already granted)
Update src/types/data-catalog.ts: add tags: string[] to Dataset interface
Update src/types/data-catalog.ts: add s3_object_key: string | null, s3_annotation_key: string | null, s3_specs_key: string | null to DatasetSample interface
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Nullable columns are backward-compatible. Existing samples use storage_path or media_url — S3 fields are for new samples only. Check existing migration files in supabase/migrations/ for naming convention.

userStories.title: Zod Schemas for Polymorphic Annotation Metadata
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Zod schemas to safely parse the three annotation metadata shapes from S3.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/schemas/annotation-metadata.ts
EgocentricShortFormSchema: z.object({ mainCategory: z.string(), subcategory: z.string() }).passthrough()
EgocentricLongFormSchema: z.object({ flow: z.string(), activities: z.array(z.string()) }).passthrough()
VideoGameSchema: z.object({ selectedGame: z.string() }).passthrough()

RekVisionMetadataSchema: z.object({ domain: z.string(), task: z.string().optional(), task_description: z.string().optional(), technical_specs: z.object({ duration_s: z.number(), resolution_px: z.object({ width: z.number(), height: z.number() }).optional(), fps_estimate: z.number().optional(), aspect_ratio: z.string().optional() }).optional(), environment_label: z.string().optional(), environment_description: z.string().optional(), hands: z.object({ hands_visible: z.boolean(), right_hand_pct: z.number(), left_hand_pct: z.number(), both_hands_pct: z.number(), primary_hand: z.string(), confidence: z.number() }).optional() }).passthrough()

ChunkingItemSchema: z.object({ start_s: z.number(), end_s: z.number(), label: z.string(), objects: z.array(z.string()).optional(), actions: z.array(z.string()).optional(), confidence: z.number().optional() })

parseAnnotationMetadata(raw: unknown) function that tries each generalData schema and returns { _type: 'short_form' | 'long_form' | 'game' | 'unknown', data: parsed } — returns _type: 'unknown' with raw data for unrecognized shapes
All schemas use .passthrough() so unknown fields are preserved
Export all schemas and types
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The three generalData shapes come from S3 annotation-data.json files. RekVision metadata comes from CSV inline metadata. Chunking is an array of timestamped segments.

userStories.title: Update Dataset Detail Page for S3 Presigned URLs
userStories.description: As a portal user, I want to view S3-hosted video samples in the dataset detail page.
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/app/portal/catalog/[id]/page.tsx signed URL resolution (around line 103-111)

New resolution order: if sample.s3_object_key → call getS3SignedUrl(sample.s3_object_key), else if sample.media_url → use directly, else if sample.storage_path → call getSignedUrl(sample.storage_path)
Import getS3SignedUrl from src/lib/s3/presigner.ts
Presigned URLs generated server-side in RSC — no client-side API calls needed for initial load
Null presigned URLs coerced to empty string (existing pattern on line 118)
No changes to VideoPlayer.tsx — it already handles video URLs
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The page.tsx already has signedUrls Promise.all pattern. Just add the s3_object_key branch first in the conditional chain.

userStories.title: MetaTable Component — Core with FIELD_LABELS
userStories.description: As a portal user, I want annotation metadata displayed in a structured, readable format instead of raw JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/portal/catalog/[id]/MetaTable.tsx as a client component
Props: { metadata: Record<string, unknown>, annotationData?: Record<string, unknown> | null }

FIELD_LABELS map defines known fields with display labels and formatters: domain (badge), task/task_description (paragraph), technical_specs.duration_s (formatted: '9s' or '3m 06s'), technical_specs.resolution_px (formatted: '1920 x 1080'), technical_specs.fps_estimate (number), technical_specs.aspect_ratio (string), environment_label (badge), environment_description (paragraph), hands.hands_visible (yes/no), hands.right_hand_pct/left_hand_pct/both_hands_pct (text percentages), hands.primary_hand (text), hands.confidence (percentage), generalData.mainCategory (badge), generalData.subcategory (badge), generalData.flow (badge), generalData.activities (comma-separated badges), generalData.selectedGame (badge)
Chunking array renders as structured text: '[0s-35s] checking_meat_tenderness (0.90)' per line
Tier 1: Known fields from FIELD_LABELS render with structured formatting. Null/empty fields silently skipped.
Tier 2: Remaining unknown fields render in collapsible 'Raw Metadata' section using JSON syntax highlighting (reuse highlightJson pattern from SampleDetailModal.tsx)
Terminal aesthetic: JetBrains Mono font, '// SECTION HEADERS' in accent-primary, dark backgrounds, existing card/badge patterns
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is the main metadata rendering component. Look at SampleDetailModal.tsx for existing highlightJson() function and extractCommonFields() helper to understand current patterns. Use existing Badge pattern from portal/catalog/[id]/page.tsx lines 45-61.

userStories.title: Integrate MetaTable and Annotation Fetch into SampleDetailModal
userStories.description: As a portal user, I want the sample detail modal to show structured metadata and fetch annotations from S3 on-demand.
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/app/portal/catalog/[id]/SampleDetailModal.tsx right panel
Replace raw JSON viewer with MetaTable component import
Pass sample.metadata_json as metadata prop to MetaTable
If sample has s3_annotation_key, trigger fetch to POST /api/portal/s3-annotation on modal open
Show loading state while annotation fetches: terminal cursor animation with 'Fetching annotation...' text
On fetch success: pass result as annotationData prop to MetaTable
On fetch failure: show only existing metadata_json in MetaTable (no error shown to user)
Keep existing extractCommonFields() summary strip above MetaTable
Keep existing 'Copy JSON' button — copies full merged metadata as raw JSON
Keep existing keyboard navigation (arrows, Escape) and click-outside-to-close
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: SampleDetailModal.tsx is a complex component with keyboard shortcuts, navigation, and media rendering. Be careful not to break existing functionality. The s3_annotation_key field is on DatasetSample type (US-004).

userStories.title: MIME-to-Renderer Registry and DownloadLink Component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a file type registry mapping MIME types to renderer components for extensible file handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/file-renderers.ts with FILE_RENDERERS record mapping MIME types to { component: string, label: string }

Registered types: video/mp4 → VideoPlayer, video/quicktime → VideoPlayer, video/webm → VideoPlayer, application/json → MetadataJsonViewer, application/gzip → DownloadLink, image/jpeg → ImageViewer, image/png → ImageViewer
getRendererForMime(mimeType: string) function: tries exact match first, then wildcard (video/* → VideoPlayer), then returns null
Create src/app/portal/catalog/[id]/DownloadLink.tsx — minimal component: terminal-styled anchor tag with download icon and file size display
Update SampleDetailModal.tsx to use getRendererForMime() instead of inline isVideoUrl()/isImageUrl() checks
Existing video and image rendering behavior unchanged — just refactored to use registry
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: SampleDetailModal.tsx already has isVideoUrl() and isImageUrl() helpers. Replace the conditional rendering logic with registry lookup. DownloadLink is a simple component.

userStories.title: Tag-Based Filtering in CatalogBrowser
userStories.description: As a portal user, I want to filter datasets by tags so I can find specific data characteristics.
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/app/portal/catalog/CatalogBrowser.tsx: add selectedTags state as string[]
Compute availableTags from all datasets' tags arrays (deduplicated, sorted alphabetically)
Render tag pill buttons below existing category pills: monospace, '#' prefix (e.g. #annotated), accent border when active
Filter logic: dataset must have ALL selected tags (AND filter)
URL sync: persist selected tags in ?tags=annotated,hand-tracking query params using URLSearchParams
Read tags from URL on mount to restore filter state
Clear all tags button visible when any tags selected
Empty state message when no datasets match: 'No datasets match the selected filters.'
Update src/app/portal/catalog/page.tsx server component to include tags in the dataset select query
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: CatalogBrowser.tsx already has searchQuery and selectedCategory state. Add selectedTags alongside. Tags come from datasets.tags column added in US-004.

userStories.title: Metadata Completeness Badges on Dataset Cards
userStories.description: As a portal user, I want visual indicators on dataset cards showing what data is available.
acceptanceCriteria: Update CatalogBrowser.tsx dataset card rendering to show badges based on dataset.tags
Badge mapping: tag 'annotated' → [ANNOTATED] in accent green (bg-[var(--accent-primary)]/10 text-[var(--accent-primary)])
Tag 'video-only' → [VIDEO] in muted (bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] text-[var(--text-muted)])
Tag 'game-specs' → [GAME SPECS] in blue (bg-blue-500/10 text-blue-400)
Tag 'hand-tracking' → [HAND TRACKING] in accent green
Badges render in card footer area, monospace, uppercase, text-[10px]
Badge visibility driven entirely by dataset.tags array — no hardcoded per-dataset logic
Datasets with no tags show no badges
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Tags are string arrays on the Dataset type. Badge component pattern exists in portal/catalog/[id]/page.tsx.

userStories.title: CSV Ingestion Script
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a script to transform the Data Catalog Samples.csv into bulk import JSON format.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/ingest-csv.ts — runs with npx tsx scripts/ingest-csv.ts
Reads Data Catalog Samples.csv from project root
Detects 4 section boundaries by header rows: 'Egocentric Crowd', 'Egocentric Workplaces', 'Video Game', 'Cinematic Licensed Data'
Each section has its own column mapping based on header row detection
For each row produces: { filename, s3_object_key, s3_annotation_key, mime_type, metadata_json: { section, type, category, subcategory, annotation_id, reka_vision_metadata?, reka_vision_chunking? } }
Extracts object_key by stripping 's3://moonvalley-annotation-platform/' prefix from S3 URIs
Handles paths with spaces, trailing spaces, mixed-case extensions (.mov .MP4 .MOV), and paths without extensions
Merges inline JSON metadata from two CSV columns (Metadata & Labeling + Chunking) into single reka_vision_metadata object
Handles 'na' or empty annotation IDs gracefully
Guesses MIME type from file extension: .mp4/.MP4 → video/mp4, .mov/.MOV → video/quicktime, .json → application/json
Outputs JSON files to scripts/output/ directory, one per dataset group (e.g. egocentric-crowd-short-form.json)
Dry-run mode with --dry-run flag: prints summary table without writing files
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CSV file is at project root: 'Data Catalog Samples.csv'. It has 4 sections separated by blank rows with different column schemas. The inline JSON in the CSV uses double-double-quotes for escaping.

userStories.title: Update Bulk Import API for S3 Fields
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the bulk import API to accept S3 object key fields so CSV script output can be imported.
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/app/api/admin/catalog/[id]/samples/bulk/route.ts to accept s3_object_key, s3_annotation_key, and s3_specs_key in sample payload
Add these fields to the Zod validation schema for the bulk import request body
Pass S3 fields through to the Supabase insert alongside existing fields (filename, media_url, metadata_json, etc.)
Existing import flow for non-S3 samples remains unchanged (backward compatible)
MIME type detection: if mime_type not provided and s3_object_key exists, guess from extension
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read the existing bulk route to understand current validation and insert patterns. The route already handles chunked insertion (100 per batch).

userStories.title: VideoPlayer Format Fallback Message
userStories.description: As a portal user, I want a clear fallback when a video format isn't supported by my browser.
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/app/portal/catalog/[id]/VideoPlayer.tsx (or the video rendering in SampleDetailModal.tsx)
Add canplay event listener on <video> element with 5-second timeout
If canplay doesn't fire within 5s OR error event fires, show fallback UI:
Fallback: terminal-styled panel with '// FORMAT NOT SUPPORTED' header, message: 'This video format may not be supported in your browser. Try Safari or download the file.', and a [Download] button
Download button links to the presigned URL with download attribute
Fallback styled: bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)], monospace, accent border, centered in video area
Also handle <video> onerror event for immediate failure detection
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This primarily affects .MOV files (QuickTime) which have inconsistent browser support. Safari supports natively, Chrome since v108, Firefox limited.

userStories.title: Format Issue Tracking API and Admin Badge
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see which video samples have format compatibility issues reported by users.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Supabase migration adding format_issues table: id uuid, sample_id uuid (FK to dataset_samples), mime_type text, user_agent text, created_at timestamptz
Create src/app/api/portal/format-issue/route.ts — POST handler: validates session, accepts { sampleId, mimeType, userAgent }, inserts row
Update VideoPlayer fallback (US-014) to POST to this endpoint when fallback is triggered
In admin sample list (src/app/admin/catalog/[id]/ area), show orange warning icon on samples that have format_issues rows
Warning icon tooltip shows count of reports
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This depends on US-014 (VideoPlayer fallback). The admin catalog pages are in src/app/admin/catalog/.

userStories.title: Annotation JSON Caching in Supabase
userStories.description: As a developer, I want fetched annotation JSONs cached in Supabase so repeat views don't re-fetch from S3.
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/app/api/portal/s3-annotation/route.ts (US-003):
Before fetching from S3: check if sample's metadata_json has _cached_at field — if yes, return metadata_json directly without S3 fetch
Need to query dataset_samples to check cache: accept sampleId in request body alongside objectKey
After successful S3 fetch: background PATCH dataset_samples.metadata_json with merged annotation data + _cached_at timestamp using admin Supabase client
Background write uses Promise but does NOT await (non-blocking to response)
If s3_annotation_key changes on the sample, cache is stale (_cached_at should be ignored) — compare objectKey param with stored s3_annotation_key
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Uses createSupabaseAdminClient() for the background write to bypass RLS. The initial response is still fast — caching happens async.

userStories.title: Data Panel Registry and Tabs Container
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an extensible panel system so different data types (annotation, game specs, future types) can be rendered as tabs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/portal/catalog/[id]/DataPanels/DataPanelRegistry.ts
Export DataPanelProps interface: { data: Record<string, unknown>, sampleId: string }
Export PANEL_REGISTRY: Record<string, { component: ComponentType<DataPanelProps>, label: string, icon: LucideIcon }>
Register 'annotation' panel type pointing to MetaTable component with FileJson icon
Register 'game_specs' panel type (component placeholder for now — will be built in US-018)
Create src/app/portal/catalog/[id]/DataPanels/DataPanelTabs.tsx — client component
Props: { panels: Array<{ type: string, data: Record<string, unknown> }>, sampleId: string }
Renders tab buttons at top for each panel in the panels array (only tabs with data)
Active tab styled with accent border-bottom, inactive tabs muted
Tab content area renders the registered component for the active tab
Terminal-styled tab bar: monospace, compact, dark background
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the extensible container. Adding a new panel type in the future = register in PANEL_REGISTRY + create component. No changes to DataPanelTabs needed.

userStories.title: GameSpecsPanel Component
userStories.description: As a portal user viewing a video game sample, I want to see game and PC specifications in a structured panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/portal/catalog/[id]/DataPanels/GameSpecsPanel.tsx implementing DataPanelProps interface

Renders structured sections: // SYSTEM (OS, CPU name, GPU name + VRAM, RAM, architecture), // GAME (game name, process name, window title), // DISPLAY (resolution, refresh rate, primary), // APPLICATION (capture app version, build date)
Each section uses terminal-styled headers (// UPPERCASE in accent color)
Field values rendered as monospace key-value pairs
Unknown fields in specs JSON fall through to collapsible 'Raw Specs' section with JSON viewer
Empty/null fields silently skipped
Register 'game_specs' in PANEL_REGISTRY (update placeholder from US-017) to point to GameSpecsPanel
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Game specs JSON structure: { System: { OperatingSystem, ProcessorName, GraphicsCards: [{ Name, VideoMemoryMB }], TotalMemoryGB }, Game: { GameName, ProcessName, WindowTitle }, Displays: [{ Width, Height, RefreshRate }], Application: { Version, BuildDate } }

userStories.title: Integrate Data Panels into SampleDetailModal
userStories.description: As a portal user, I want the sample detail modal right panel to use the extensible DataPanelTabs system.
acceptanceCriteria: Update SampleDetailModal.tsx right panel to use DataPanelTabs instead of direct MetaTable
Build panels array dynamically from sample data: always include 'annotation' panel if metadata exists, include 'game_specs' panel if sample has specs data in metadata_json or s3_specs_key
If sample has s3_specs_key, fetch specs JSON on-demand (same pattern as annotation fetch — POST to /api/portal/s3-annotation with the specs key)
Single-tab case: don't show tab bar, just render the panel content directly
Multi-tab case: show tab bar with panel labels and icons
Keep existing Copy JSON button below the tabs — copies all panel data as merged JSON
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This connects US-017 (DataPanelTabs) and US-018 (GameSpecsPanel) into the existing modal. Be careful not to break the annotation fetch flow from US-008.

Terminal-styled file upload dropzone accepting .csv files

Display detected sections with row counts in a summary panel

userStories.title: Admin CSV Import — Column Mapping UI
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to map CSV columns to dataset fields for each detected section.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Step 2 UI to admin import page (src/app/admin/catalog/import/page.tsx)
For each detected CSV section, show column mapping form:
Dropdown per target field: video_s3_key, annotation_s3_key, specs_s3_key, type, category, subcategory, annotation_id, metadata_json
Each dropdown lists the CSV column headers from that section
Auto-suggest mappings based on column header text (e.g. 'S3 URI - Video' → video_s3_key)

Dataset selector per section: dropdown of existing datasets or 'Create new' option
Step navigation: Back to Step 1, Next to Step 3
Mapping state stored in component state (not persisted)

Show summary: 'X samples ready, Y warnings, Z errors'
Add Step 4 (Execute Import) as final step:
Submit button calls existing bulk import API (POST /api/admin/catalog/[id]/samples/bulk) per dataset
Progress indicator showing import status per dataset
Results summary: 'X imported, Y skipped, Z failed' with error details displayed
Reset button to start over

userStories.title: Playwright E2E — Catalog Browse and Filter Flow
userStories.description: As QA, I need an end-to-end test verifying catalog browse, category filtering, tag filtering, and search.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/e2e/catalog-browse.spec.ts
Test setup: navigate to /portal/catalog authenticated as test lead (john+client@claru.ai)
Verify dataset cards render on the page
Click a category filter pill — verify filtered results change
Select a tag filter — verify AND filtering with category
Type in search box — verify results filter by query
Clear all filters — verify full list restores
Verify URL query params update with each filter change
Refresh page — verify filters persist from URL params
Verify metadata completeness badges visible on cards
Test uses Playwright MCP browser automation tools
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Test lead john+client@claru.ai has Supabase auth (magic link). For Playwright MCP, use mcp__playwright__browser_navigate, mcp__playwright__browser_click, mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot, etc. Dev server must be running on localhost:3000.

userStories.title: Playwright E2E — Sample Detail and Video Playback Flow
userStories.description: As QA, I need an end-to-end test verifying S3 video playback and metadata display in the sample detail modal.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/e2e/sample-detail.spec.ts
Test setup: navigate to /portal/catalog/[dataset-id] authenticated as test lead
Verify sample gallery grid renders
Click first sample — verify detail modal opens
Verify video element exists with src containing presigned URL (X-Amz-Signature in URL)
Verify MetaTable section renders (look for '// DOMAIN' or '// TASK' section headers if metadata exists)
Verify Copy JSON button exists and is clickable
Navigate to next sample via arrow button — verify modal content updates
Close modal via Escape key — verify return to gallery
Open a sample without annotation data — verify no stuck loading spinner
Test uses Playwright MCP browser automation tools
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Presigned URLs contain X-Amz-Signature query parameter — check for this to verify S3 integration. Use mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot to inspect DOM.

userStories.title: Playwright E2E — Full User Acceptance Flow
userStories.description: As QA, I need a comprehensive acceptance test covering the full user journey from browse to playback to metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/e2e/user-acceptance.spec.ts
Complete user journey test:
1. Navigate to /portal/catalog as authenticated lead
2. Verify all dataset categories visible
3. Filter to a specific category
4. Select a tag filter
5. Open a dataset from filtered results
6. Verify stats bar shows sample count
7. Open first sample in detail modal
8. Verify video element loads (src is presigned URL)
9. Verify structured metadata visible in MetaTable
10. Navigate through 2 more samples using keyboard or buttons
11. Close modal and go back to catalog
12. Clear filters and search for a specific term
13. Open a different dataset and verify it loads
Take screenshots at key steps for visual baseline
Test uses Playwright MCP browser automation tools
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the comprehensive acceptance test. It validates the entire integration end-to-end. Screenshots saved to tests/e2e/screenshots/.
